candidateAtoms.subject: ts-admin-basic-info-service.AdminBasicInfoService
candidateAtoms.object: declares Response-returning CRUD methods over Contacts, Station, TrainType, Config, PriceInfo resources

candidateAtoms.subject: ts-admin-order-service.AdminOrderService
candidateAtoms.object: declares Response-returning getAllOrders/deleteOrder/updateOrder/addOrder methods over Order resource

candidateAtoms.subject: ts-admin-route-service.AdminRouteService
candidateAtoms.object: declares Response-returning getAllRoutes/createAndModifyRoute/deleteRoute methods over RouteInfo resource

candidateContexts.label: ts-admin-order-service admin gateway facade (order/order-other train-number-routed proxy)
ts-admin-route-service admin gateway facade (route create/modify with station-existence check)
shared boundary where the three admin facades call resource-owning backing services via RestTemplate

candidateCovers.label: admin gateway convention measurement surface across basic-info/order/route admin services and their shared downstream boundary

notes: Spring Boot bootstrap class only; declares a load-balanced RestTemplate bean, no domain logic.

Read-only endpoints for stations/trains/prices/configs/contacts are permitted without authentication; the rest of the service path requires ADMIN role; CORS bean allows all origins/methods/headers with credentials disabled.

21 endpoints proxy straight to AdminBasicInfoService; every mutation and every listing except /welcome carries @CrossOrigin(origins="*") at the method level, duplicating the wildcard CORS bean in SecurityConfig.

basicPriceRate and firstClassPriceRate are plain double fields (rate multipliers, not a raw currency amount) — recorded as a money-amount convention observation since it is the numeric-representation evidence available in this chunk.
Pure interface; 20 Response-returning CRUD method declarations, no bodies to survey for behavior.
Every method is a thin RestTemplate proxy to one of five downstream services; DiscoveryClient is injected but never invoked in this file (declaration-only dependency).
Declares port 18767, nacos discovery cluster address, and application name ts-admin-basic-info-service.
Spring Boot bootstrap class only, identical shape to the basic-info application class.
Unlike basic-info, no GET routes are carved out as permitAll here; the whole /api/v1/adminorderservice/** path requires ADMIN role.
Only getAllOrders carries @CrossOrigin; addOrder/updateOrder/deleteOrder do not, unlike the uniform per-endpoint CORS pattern in AdminBasicInfoController.
Pure interface, four Response-returning method declarations.

trainNumber starting with G or D consistently routes to ts-order-service, otherwise to ts-order-other-service, across delete/update/add; getAllOrders merges results from both providers unconditionally.
Declares port 16112, same nacos discovery cluster address, application name ts-admin-order-service.
Spring Boot bootstrap class only, identical shape to the other admin application classes.
Same shape as the order service's SecurityConfig: whole path gated to ADMIN, no per-route permitAll carve-outs.
Only getAllRoutes carries @CrossOrigin; addRoute and deleteRoute do not, the same asymmetric CORS pattern seen in AdminOrderController.
Pure interface, three Response-returning method declarations.

createAndModifyRoute validates start/end station membership locally before calling checkStationsExists, which itself calls ts-station-service; three call sites compare ResponseEntity status against HttpStatus.ACCEPTED and only log when it differs, never branch on it.
Declares port 16113, same nacos discovery cluster address, application name ts-admin-route-service.
Spring Boot bootstrap class only; no controller, service, config, or resources file for this service fell within the assigned chunk.
